notes.note: A quartan fever recurs every fourth day (counting inclusively); ancient medicine regarded the shift to this milder, regular type as a sign the illness was abating.

notes.anchor: the day before the Nones of January
notes.note: 4 January 49 BC. The Nones fell on the 5th in January, so the day before is the 4th.

notes.anchor: I have walked into the very flame of civil discord
notes.note: Cicero returned from his Cilician governorship to the outskirts of Rome on the eve of the civil war between Caesar and Pompey, which broke out within days.

Mark Antony and Quintus Cassius Longinus, the two tribunes of 49 BC who, having vetoed the senate's measures against Caesar, fled the city to join him -- the pretext Caesar used for crossing the Rubicon.

notes.anchor: to see to it that the commonwealth take no harm
notes.note: The senatus consultum ultimum, the senate's emergency decree empowering the magistrates to use any means to defend the state; passed on 7 January 49 BC.

notes.anchor: the senate, in a full house, has all the same demanded the triumph
notes.note: Cicero had been voted a supplicatio for his Cilician victory and hoped for a triumph; the request was repeatedly deferred amid the crisis and never granted.
Capua in Campania was secured as a Pompeian base and mustering-ground; Cicero himself was assigned charge of the Campanian coast.
